title: clinopodium acinos subsp. villosum
intro: Clinopodium acinos subsp. villosum, also known as alpine calamint, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to mountainous regions of Europe. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate digestive issues, soothe respiratory problems, and support immune function. The health benefits of alpine calamint include its ability to ease symptoms of colds and flu, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve digestion. Alpine calamint can be prepared as a herbal tea, infused oil, or dried herb for use in traditional medicine, and is also used as a culinary ingredient in some recipes.
